page: Google Cloud Platform n4a-highcpu-1 is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine instance featuring 1 dedicated vCPU and 2.0 GB of system memory. This configuration yields a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU. The instance does not include bundled local storage or integrated GPU hardware, and it is provisioned with zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

With its dedicated CPU allocation, this instance provides predictable single-core compute performance without the resource-sharing overhead typical of burstable tiers. The 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio positions it as a compute-focused option for lightweight workloads. It offers a cost-effective entry point for tasks requiring dedicated CPU execution but minimal memory overhead, such as simple microservices, lightweight background workers, basic utility scripting, and small-scale automation tasks.
description: Google Cloud Platform n4a-highcpu-1 is an x86_64 virtual machine instance configured with 1 dedicated vCPU and 2.0 GB of system memory. Lacking local storage and GPU accelerators, this instance focuses entirely on providing a dedicated single-core compute environment with a 2.0 GB memory-to-core ratio. The dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent execution performance, distinguishing it from shared-core or burstable alternatives. This resource profile is optimized for lightweight, compute-oriented tasks that do not demand large memory footprints. It serves as a cost-effective solution for running simple microservices, background workers, automation scripts, and basic utility workloads requiring predictable CPU access.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n4a-highcpu-1 is an x86_64 virtual machine featuring 1 dedicated vCPU and 2.0 GB of RAM, designed for lightweight compute workloads requiring predictable single-core performance.

tagline: A dedicated single-core virtual machine with a compute-focused memory ratio for predictable execution of lightweight utility workloads.

Consistent single-core performance without burstable CPU credits
